mn14:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Sakyans at Kapilavatthu in Nigrodha’s Park.
mn14:10.1: Again, Mahānāma, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause, kings quarrel with kings, nobles quarrel with nobles, brahmins quarrel with brahmins, householders quarrel with householders; mother quarrels with son, son quarrels with mother, father quarrels with son, son quarrels with father, brother quarrels with brother, brother quarrels with sister, sister quarrels with brother, friend quarrels with friend.
mn14:10.2: There they engage in quarrels, brawls, and disputes, and attack one another with fists, clods, sticks, and weapons.
mn14:10.3: There they meet with death, or *dukkha* tantamount to death.
mn14:10.4: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:11.1: Again, Mahānāma, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause, taking up sword and shield, and strapping on bow and quiver, they charge into battle with both sides massed, while arrows are shot, spears are hurled, and swords flash.
mn14:11.2: There they are pierced by arrows, pierced by spears, and their heads are cut off with swords.
mn14:11.3: There they meet with death, or *dukkha* tantamount to death.
mn14:11.4: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:12.1: Again, Mahānāma, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause, taking up sword and shield, and strapping on bow and quiver, they assault ramparts smeared with wet clay, while arrows are shot, spears are hurled, and swords flash.
mn14:12.2: There they are pierced by arrows, pierced by spears, drenched even with dung, crushed with heavy blows, and their heads are cut off with swords.
mn14:12.3: There they meet with death, or *dukkha* tantamount to death.
mn14:12.4: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:13.1: Again, Mahānāma, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause, they break through walls, carry off plunder, commit burglary, lie in ambush on the roads, and go after another’s wife.
mn14:13.2: Kings arrest them and inflict various punishments on them—
mn14:13.3: they flog them with whips, flog them with canes, flog them with clubs; they cut off a hand, cut off a foot, cut off hand and foot, cut off an ear, cut off the nose, cut off ear and nose; they inflict the porridge-pot, the shell-shave, Rāhu’s-mouth, the fire-garland, the flaming-hand, the bark-twist, the rag-dress, the antelope, the meat-hooks, the coin-cuts, the lye-pickle, the bar-turn, the straw-seat; they pour hot oil over them, have them devoured by dogs, impale them alive on stakes, and cut off their heads with swords.
mn14:13.4: There they meet with death, or *dukkha* tantamount to death.
mn14:13.5: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:14.1: Again, Mahānāma, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause, they engage in misconduct by body, engage in misconduct by speech, engage in misconduct by mind [*citta*].
mn14:14.2: Having engaged in misconduct by body, speech, and mind [*citta*], with the breakup of the body, after death, they are reborn in a plane of loss, a bad destination, a downfall, hell.
mn14:14.3: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures concerning the next life, a mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:15.1: Once, Mahānāma, I was dwelling at Rājagaha on Mount Gijjhakūṭa.
mn14:15.2: At that time, many Nigaṇṭhas on the Black Rock on the side of Isigili were standing, rejecting seats, experiencing self-inflicted painful, severe, harsh, sharp *vedanā*s.
mn14:16.1: Then, Mahānāma, in the evening I came out of retreat and went to the Black Rock on the side of Isigili, to those Nigaṇṭhas. Having gone there, I said this to them:
mn14:16.2: ‘Why is it, friends Nigaṇṭhas, that you are standing, rejecting seats, experiencing self-inflicted painful, severe, harsh, sharp *vedanā*s?’
mn14:17.1: When this was said, Mahānāma, those Nigaṇṭhas said this to me:
mn14:17.2: ‘Friend, Nigaṇṭha Nāṭaputta claims to be all-knowing and all-seeing, with knowledge and vision complete in every respect:
mn14:17.3: “Whether I am walking or standing or sleeping or awake, knowledge and vision are continuously and constantly present to me.”
mn14:17.5: “There is, Nigaṇṭhas, bad *kamma* [*pāpakammaṁ*] done by you in the past. Exhaust that through this bitter practice of austerity.
mn14:17.6: And whatever there is here and now of restraint in body, restraint in speech, restraint in mind [*citta*], that is the non-doing of bad *kamma* [*pāpassa kammassa*] in the future.
mn14:17.7: Thus, through austerity there is the bringing to an end of old *kamma*s, and by the non-doing of new *kamma*s there is no influx in the future;
mn14:17.8: with no influx in the future comes the exhaustion of *kamma* [*kammakkhayo*]; with the exhaustion of *kamma* comes the exhaustion of *dukkha* [*dukkhakkhayo*]; with the exhaustion of *dukkha* comes the exhaustion of *vedanā* [*vedanākkhayo*]; with the exhaustion of *vedanā*, all *dukkha* will be worn away.”
mn14:17.9: And this pleases us and is acceptable to us; therefore we are satisfied with it.’
mn14:18.1: When this was said, Mahānāma, I said this to those Nigaṇṭhas:
mn14:18.12: “This much *dukkha* has been worn away, this much *dukkha* is yet to be worn away, and when this much *dukkha* has been worn away, all *dukkha* will be worn away”?’
mn14:18.15: “the abandoning of unwholesome *dhammas* and the attainment of wholesome *dhammas* here and now”?’
mn14:18.3: “We certainly existed in the past; it is not the case that we did not exist”?’
mn14:18.6: “We certainly did bad *kamma* [*pāpakammaṁ*] in the past; it is not the case that we did not do it”?’
mn14:19.1: ‘So then, friends Nigaṇṭhas, you do not know—
mn14:19.2: “We certainly existed in the past; it is not the case that we did not exist”; you do not know—
mn14:19.3: “We certainly did bad *kamma* [*pāpakammaṁ*] in the past; it is not the case that we did not do it”; you do not know—
mn14:19.4: “We did such-and-such bad *kamma* [*pāpakammaṁ*]”; you do not know—
mn14:19.5: “This much *dukkha* has been worn away, this much *dukkha* is yet to be worn away, and when this much *dukkha* has been worn away, all *dukkha* will be worn away.”
mn14:19.7: the abandoning of unwholesome *dhammas* and the attainment of wholesome *dhammas* here and now.
mn14:19.8: That being so, friends Nigaṇṭhas, it would seem that those in the world who are brutal, bloody-handed, engaged in cruel deeds, when reborn among humans, go forth among the Nigaṇṭhas.’
mn14:2.1: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to the *Bhagavā*, he paid homage to him and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Mahānāma the Sakyan said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn14:2.2: “For a long time, *bhante*, I have understood the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā* in this way:
mn14:2.3: ‘Greed is a corruption of the mind [*citta*], hate is a corruption of the mind [*citta*], delusion is a corruption of the mind [*citta*].’
mn14:2.4: And, *bhante*, I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā* in this way:
mn14:2.5: ‘Greed is a corruption of the mind [*citta*], hate is a corruption of the mind [*citta*], delusion is a corruption of the mind [*citta*].’
mn14:2.6: Yet still, from time to time, states of greed take over my mind [*citta*] and remain; states of hate take over my mind [*citta*] and remain; states of delusion take over my mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn14:2.8: ‘What *dhamma* has not been abandoned internally in me, because of which, from time to time, states of greed take over my mind [*citta*] and remain, states of hate take over my mind [*citta*] and remain, and states of delusion take over my mind [*citta*] and remain?’”
mn14:20.1: ‘No, friend Gotama, *sukha* is not to be gained through *sukha*; rather, *sukha* is to be gained through *dukkha*.
mn14:20.10: But let that be. Even now we ask Venerable Gotama:
mn14:20.11: “Which of the two dwells more pleasantly: King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha, or Venerable Gotama?”
mn14:20.2: For if, friend Gotama, *sukha* were to be gained through *sukha*, then King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha would gain *sukha*; King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha dwells more pleasantly than Venerable Gotama.’
mn14:20.3: ‘Surely the venerable Nigaṇṭhas have spoken rashly and without reflection:
mn14:20.4: “No, friend Gotama, *sukha* is not to be gained through *sukha*; rather, *sukha* is to be gained through *dukkha*.
mn14:20.5: For if, friend Gotama, *sukha* were to be gained through *sukha*, then King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha would gain *sukha*; King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha dwells more pleasantly than Venerable Gotama.”
mn14:20.6: But rather I should be questioned there:
mn14:20.7: “Which of the two dwells more pleasantly: King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha, or Venerable Gotama?”
mn14:20.8: ‘Indeed, friend Gotama, we have spoken rashly and without reflection: “No, friend Gotama, *sukha* is not to be gained through *sukha*; rather, *sukha* is to be gained through *dukkha*.
mn14:20.9: For if, friend Gotama, *sukha* were to be gained through *sukha*, then King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha would gain *sukha*; King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha dwells more pleasantly than Venerable Gotama.”
mn14:21.1: Then, friends Nigaṇṭhas, I will question you in return. Answer it as seems fit to you.
mn14:21.11: one day and night to dwell experiencing pure *sukha*?’
mn14:21.13: ‘But I, friends Nigaṇṭhas, am able, without moving my body and without uttering speech, to dwell for one day and night experiencing pure *sukha*.
mn14:21.14: I, friends Nigaṇṭhas, am able, without moving my body and without uttering speech, for two days and nights …
mn14:21.19: seven days and nights to dwell experiencing pure *sukha*.
mn14:21.2: What do you think, friends Nigaṇṭhas? Is King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha able,
mn14:21.20: What do you think, friends Nigaṇṭhas? That being so, which of the two dwells more pleasantly: King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha, or I?’
mn14:21.21: ‘That being so, Venerable Gotama dwells more pleasantly than King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha.’”
mn14:21.23: Satisfied, Mahānāma the Sakyan rejoiced in what the *Bhagavā* had said.
mn14:21.24: The Shorter Mass of *dukkha*, the fourth, is finished.
mn14:21.3: without moving his body and without uttering speech, to dwell for seven days and nights experiencing pure *sukha*?”
mn14:21.5: ‘What do you think, friends Nigaṇṭhas? Is King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha able,
mn14:21.6: without moving his body and without uttering speech, for six days and nights …
mn14:3.1: “That very *dhamma*, Mahānāma, has not been abandoned internally in you, because of which, from time to time, states of greed take over your mind [*citta*] and remain, states of hate take over your mind [*citta*] and remain, and states of delusion take over your mind [*citta*] and remain.
mn14:3.2: And if that *dhamma* had been abandoned internally in you, Mahānāma, you would not be living the household life, and you would not be enjoying sensual pleasures.
mn14:3.3: But because that very *dhamma* has not been abandoned internally in you, Mahānāma, you are living the household life and enjoying sensual pleasures.
mn14:4.1: ‘Sensual pleasures give little gratification and much *dukkha*, much distress; the drawback here is greater’—
mn14:4.2: even if an ariyan disciple has well seen this as it truly is with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*], yet if he does not attain *pīti* and *sukha* [*pītisukhaṁ*] quite apart from sensual pleasures, apart from unwholesome *dhammas*, or something more peaceful than that;
mn14:4.3: still, he is one who returns to sensual pleasures.
mn14:4.4: But when, Mahānāma, an ariyan disciple has well seen this as it truly is with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*]: ‘Sensual pleasures give little gratification and much *dukkha*, much distress; the drawback here is greater,’ and he does attain *pīti* and *sukha* [*pītisukhaṁ*] quite apart from sensual pleasures, apart from unwholesome *dhammas*, or something more peaceful than that;
mn14:4.5: then he is one who does not return to sensual pleasures.
mn14:5.1: Before my awakening too, Mahānāma, while I was still not fully awakened, being only the bodhisatta,
mn14:5.2: I had well seen this as it truly is with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*]: ‘Sensual pleasures give little gratification and much *dukkha*, much distress; the drawback here is greater,’
mn14:5.3: but I did not attain *pīti* and *sukha* [*pītisukhaṁ*] quite apart from sensual pleasures, apart from unwholesome *dhammas*, or something more peaceful than that;
mn14:5.4: still, I did not regard myself as one who does not return to sensual pleasures.
mn14:5.5: But when, Mahānāma, I had well seen this as it truly is with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*]: ‘Sensual pleasures give little gratification and much *dukkha*, much distress; the drawback here is greater,’ and I did attain *pīti* and *sukha* [*pītisukhaṁ*] quite apart from sensual pleasures, apart from unwholesome *dhammas*, or something more peaceful than that;
mn14:5.6: then I regarded myself as one who does not return to sensual pleasures.
mn14:6.1: And what, Mahānāma, is the gratification in sensual pleasures?
mn14:6.10: Whatever *sukha* and gladness arises dependent on these five strands of sensual pleasures—
mn14:6.11: this is the gratification in sensual pleasures.
mn14:6.2: These are the five strands of sensual pleasures, Mahānāma.
mn14:6.4: Forms cognizable by the eye that are desirable, lovely, pleasing, agreeable, connected with sensual pleasures, enticing;
mn14:6.8: tactile objects cognizable by the body that are desirable, lovely, pleasing, agreeable, connected with sensual pleasures, enticing—
mn14:6.9: these are the five strands of sensual pleasures, Mahānāma.
mn14:7.1: And what, Mahānāma, is the drawback of sensual pleasures?
mn14:7.2: Here, Mahānāma, a clansman makes a living by some craft—
mn14:7.3: whether by bookkeeping, or by accounting, or by calculating, or by farming, or by trade, or by cattle-tending, or by archery, or by royal service, or by some other craft,
mn14:7.4: he is assailed by cold, assailed by heat, hurt by contact with flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and creeping things, dying from hunger and thirst;
mn14:7.5: this too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:8.1: If, Mahānāma, while that clansman is thus striving, making an effort, and exerting himself, that wealth does not materialize,
mn14:8.2: he sorrows, grows weary, laments, beats his breast and wails, and falls into confusion: ‘Surely my striving is in vain; surely my effort is fruitless.’
mn14:8.3: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
mn14:9.1: But if, Mahānāma, while that clansman is thus striving, making an effort, and exerting himself, that wealth does materialize,
mn14:9.2: he experiences pain and dejection on account of guarding that wealth:
mn14:9.3: ‘How can it be that kings would not seize my wealth, thieves would not seize it, fire would not burn it, water would not sweep it away, and unloved heirs would not take it?’
mn14:9.4: While he thus guards and protects that wealth, kings seize it, or thieves seize it, or fire burns it, or water sweeps it away, or unloved heirs take it.
mn14:9.5: He sorrows, grows weary, laments, beats his breast and wails, and falls into confusion:
mn14:9.6: ‘What I had is now no more.’
mn14:9.7: This too, Mahānāma, is a drawback of sensual pleasures, a directly visible mass of *dukkha*, because of sensual pleasures, with sensual pleasures as source, sensual pleasures as basis, with sensual pleasures themselves as the cause.
